bridge.note: The rare trap made of nothing: Italian needs a preposition (da solo) exactly where Spanish needs none. And agreement still applies on both sides — da sola, sola. The hardest flips to learn are the ones where one language is silent.

interference.es_to_it: The trap is an ABSENCE: Spanish uses the bare adjective (lo hizo solo), so Spanish speakers drop the da — but Italian requires it: l'ha fatto DA solo. The bare 'solo' in Italian reads as 'only'.
interference.it_to_es: Italians import the da: 'lo hizo de solo' — not Spanish. Bare solo/sola carries it (a solas = in private, a different phrase).

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
